title: Research Scientist

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run literature synthesis, propose experiment variants, and draft manuscript sections, but designing the core hypothesis and validating results experimentally stay with the scientist.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI research-assistant tools (literature review, hypothesis generation, lab-notebook copilots) are mature and widely used in industry R&D.
rationales.adoption: Large pharma, biotech, and industrial R&D labs have adopted AI research assistants broadly; smaller labs and academia trail.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run full literature-to-draft-experiment loops and manage lab-notebook documentation, but experimental execution and validation need a scientist.

tasksAtRisk: Literature review synthesis and gap-analysis drafting
tasksGrowing: Core hypothesis formulation and research direction-setting
Cross-team scientific leadership and grant strategy
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 19-1042, p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI research assistants now draft literature reviews and propose experiment variants, but deciding which hypothesis is worth testing, and validating the result, is still a scientist's call.
